strengths: Successfully completed the circle cutting task in 89.3 seconds
Maintained good traction throughout the cutting process
Demonstrated consistent scissor control during circle following
Achieved clean circle release without major deviations
weaknesses: Inconsistent entry angle positioning at the gauze edge
Minor line deviation during circle following phase

drills.name: Edge-entry rehearsal
drills.description: 15 starts from the gauze edge to a marked line without overshoot, focusing on optimal scissor entry angle
drills.targets_weakness: Inconsistent entry angle positioning at the gauze edge

drills.name: Traction-angle practice
drills.description: 10 repetitions of repositioning the Maryland to improve scissor entry angle while maintaining steady traction

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction to improve line following precision
drills.targets_weakness: Minor line deviation during circle following phase

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your consistent completion ability into more precise line following and smoother traction transitions. Your completion time of 89.3 seconds shows good efficiency - now work on eliminating the minor deviations to approach advanced-level precision.
